records.excerpt: That equation at the top is one of the famous “Ramanujan-type” series for ( \tfrac{1}{\pi} ) (a hypergeometric series that converges ridiculously fast): [\frac{1}{\pi}=\frac{2\sqrt{2}}{9801}\sum_{k=0}^{\infty}\frac{(4k)!,(1103+26390k)}{(k!)^4,396^{4k}}.] What it’s saying (in plain terms) It expresses (1/\pi) as an infinite sum. Each term uses factorials ((4k)!) and ((k!)^4), and a huge power (396^{4k}) in the denominator. That (396^{4k}) makes terms shrink very fast, so you get many correct digits of (\pi) from only a few terms. How fast does it converge? Empirically, this series gives about ~8 digits of (\pi) per additional term. Here’s what you get if you stop after a few terms (i.e., partial sums): Using only (k=0) (1 term):[\pi \approx 3.1415927300133056603\ldots] Using (k=0,1) (2 terms):[\pi \approx 3.1415926535897938779\ldots] Using (k=0,1,2) (3 terms):[\pi \approx 3.141592653589793238462649\ldots] True (\pi) begins:[3.141592653589793238462643383\ldots] So 2 terms already nail ~15 digits, and 3 terms push beyond ~20 digits. Why this works at all (high-level “where it comes from”) These (1/\pi) series come out of deep connections between: hypergeometric series, elliptic integrals, and modular functions / modular equations (special values that make everything “lock in” to (\pi) with huge cancellation and insane convergence). records.excerpt: THE UNIFIED CYCLICAL TIME SYSTEM Root Cycles, Archetypal Energies, and the Fractal Nature of Time A Synthesis of Vedic, Mayan, Western, Chinese, Egyptian, and Mesopotamian Time-Wisdom THE MASTER PRINCIPLE Time is not a line. Time is a spiral. The same archetypal energies repeat at every scale—from cosmic ages spanning millions of years to the hours of a single day. What the ancients called "astrology" is the science of reading this spiral at the appropriate magnification. Every culture that developed sophisticated time-tracking discovered the same underlying structure. They gave it different names, different mythologies, different numbers—but the PATTERN is identical. This document reveals that pattern. PART I: THE ROOT CYCLES All smaller cycles are zoom levels into these master patterns. The Root Cycles describe the largest-scale rhythms of consciousness, civilization, and cosmic evolution. These are not predictions of literal events—they are descriptions of the QUALITY of time, the archetypal "weather" through which all events unfold. A. THE VEDIC MAHA YUGA (4,320,000 Years) The Maha Yuga is the complete cycle of consciousness descending from pure spirit into dense matter, then ascending back. records.excerpt: Alright, this is a really good pairing of images — they’re pointing at the same idea from two different angles:one geometric, one calculus/topological. Let me unpack it cleanly, no fluff. Image 1: the stacked cube with √2, √3, √4, √5, √6 This diagram is showing how square-roots emerge naturally from dimensional movement. Start with: A unit length 1 on the base. Each red diagonal is not arbitrary — it’s the Euclidean distance created by moving orthogonally in more dimensions. What’s happening step-by-step: √2: diagonal across a square→ move 1 unit in x, 1 unit in y→ √(1² + 1²) √3: diagonal across a cube→ move 1 in x, y, z→ √(1² + 1² + 1²) √4 = 2: straight vertical (two stacked units) √5, √6: diagonals that combine vertical + horizontal movement→ these are Pythagorean extensions into higher stacked dimensions Key idea: Each √n is a projection of a straight line through higher-dimensional space. So irrational numbers aren’t “weird” — they’re just shadows of higher-dimensional straightness. Image 2: the “CALC III creature” This is doing the exact same thing, but with fields instead of lengths. Calc III is where math stops being about numbers and starts being about flow through space.
